expectations.text: Status values defined with as const and type derived via typeof, not a hand-written union
expectations.evidence: PaymentStatus is declared as a hand-written union type: export type PaymentStatus = 'pending' | 'completed' | 'failed' | 'refunded'. No as const object is used and no typeof derivation is present.

expectations.text: Return type is a specific union (e.g. BadgeColor), not string
expectations.evidence: getPaymentBadgeColor is typed as returning string. No narrower union type (e.g. BadgeColor) is defined or used as the return type.

expectations.text: No runtime default fallback value -- missing cases are compile errors only
expectations.evidence: BADGE_COLORS is a Record<PaymentStatus, string> with all four statuses covered. The function returns BADGE_COLORS[status] directly with no fallback. Missing keys are caught at compile time by the Record constraint.

expectations.text: Uses Record<Status, Color> or exhaustive switch with never check
expectations.evidence: BADGE_COLORS is typed as Record<PaymentStatus, string>, which forces all members of PaymentStatus to be present at compile time.

expectations.text: Uses named export, not default export
expectations.evidence: Both getPaymentBadgeColor and PaymentBadge use named exports (export function). No default export is present.

assertion_critique: Assertion 1 failure: The output uses a literal union type directly instead of deriving it from an as const object via typeof.
Assertion 2 failure: The color return type is widened to string rather than a constrained union like 'yellow' | 'green' | 'red' | 'blue'.
